title: Feature: add "--squash-older-than DATE" argument to buildah commit
body:
Buildah commit currently only support a `--squash` argument to produce an image with one layer. This issue is about a feature to implement a `--squash-older-than AGE` or `--squash-max-layers N` argument to produce an image that only keeps recent layers.

The use-case is for using an image similarly to a regular fedora workstation runtime:

Initialization:
```bash
ctx = $(buildah from fedora)
buildah commit $ctx local-fedora
buildah delete $ctx
```

Package installation: `layer(intermediate-base)`
```bash
ctx = $(buildah from local-fedora)
buildah run $ctx dnf install -y firefox
buildah commit $ctx local-fedora
buildah delete $ctx
```

Then periodically update the image: `layer(intermediate-base)` + (N-1) * `layer(update)` + `layer(update)`
```bash
ctx = $(buildah from local-fedora)
buildah run $ctx dnf update -y
buildah commit $ctx local-fedora:$(date "+%Y-%m-%d")
buildah delete $ctx
buildah tag local-fedora:$(date "+%Y-%m-%d") local-fedora:latest
```

The issue with that approach is that the layers are never removed and passing a certain numbers, things start to get slower and slower. Using a `--squash-older-than` argument would enable a more efficient image while keeping the regular dnf update in-place workflow.

Another solution discussed on IRC is that the `layer(update)` is never re-used and instead produced each time from `layer(intermediate-base)`. This can be optimized using `-v /var/cache/dnf:/var/cache/dnf` when updating.
Though there is a performance cost because daily update may update the same package over and over until the `layer(intermediate-base)` is rebuilt cleanly

comments.body: I like the thought, I'm just not sure how well squishing some but not all layers will work code wise.

Given that layers are build on top of each other I would assume that the layer digests would all change when lower levels are being squashed. I would be more interested in just squashing some top layers. This way I can still have a common set of base layers and just squash some later layers together. The current squash implementation results in no reuse of layers between images at all.
@Hendrik-H yes, this is also mentioned in https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/1790#issuecomment-534579845
Ok lets see about moving forward on this.
Is anyone going to work on this in the community, if not I will close in a month.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
